consistency_check_vs_a1_a2: agree: A.2 identified mutex_atomic as dominant (5.73× growth). THREADSAFE=1 + MUTEX_PTHREADS confirms SQLite uses pthreads mutexes internally. With 8 threads and 8 readers in ReaderPool, the pool Mutex itself is a contention point — all threads compete to borrow/release via Mutex<Vec<Connection>>. The proxy counter shows search_us_per_query=542µs concurrent with embedder~0µs, meaning borrow+read dominates. This is consistent with A.2 mutex_atomic signal. strace futex count unavailable but THREADSAFE=1 pthreads path is confirmed.
data_for_pivot: THREADSAFE=1 (MUTEX_PTHREADS) confirmed. A.2 mutex_atomic 5.73× growth + THREADSAFE=1 together point clearly at MULTITHREAD (THREADSAFE=2) upgrade as the targeted intervention: SQLite SERIALIZED mode uses a global recursive mutex per connection; switching to MULTITHREAD removes that per-connection serialization. ReaderPool has 8 connections and 8 threads — pool size is not the bottleneck, but the Mutex<Vec<Connection>> in ReaderPool::borrow/release adds Rust-side contention on top of SQLite's own per-connection mutex. B.1 should address both layers. The canonical_lookup full-scan (no index on write_cursor) is a secondary latency contributor that will grow with data volume — worth a follow-up but not the primary bottleneck at current fixture size.
unexpected_observations: 1) canonical_nodes has no index on write_cursor — a full table scan for each vec0 match result. Current fixture is 4 rows so invisible in perf, but this will degrade at scale. 2) DEFAULT_MMAP_SIZE=0 — no memory-mapped I/O configured; all reads go through the SQLite page cache (DEFAULT_CACHE_SIZE=-2000, ~8MB per connection × 9 connections = ~72MB total potential cache). 3) DEFAULT_SYNCHRONOUS=2 (FULL) — no WAL-specific reduction; writes are sync-heavy but reads are unaffected. 4) reader_pragmas show mmap_size=0 confirming no mmap on readers. 5) embedder_us=23µs total across 1600 queries (0µs/query) — embedder is entirely off the critical path for RoutedEmbedder; real production embedder latency would change this picture significantly.
next_phase_recommendation: Feeds A4 decision: THREADSAFE=1 confirmed (baseline for B.1 MULTITHREAD=2 target). mutex_atomic A.2 verdict corroborated by MUTEX_PTHREADS compile option. strace unavailable but not blocking — A.2 flamegraph evidence is sufficient. A.4 can proceed with: THREADSAFE=1 baseline, mutex_atomic dominant, canonical_lookup missing-index as secondary observation. B.1 should target sqlite3_config(SQLITE_CONFIG_MULTITHREAD) + potentially add write_cursor index on canonical_nodes.
